strengths: 10+ years full-stack TypeScript/Node.js development across enterprise and startup contexts, significantly exceeding 6+ year requirement with proven microservices architecture, performance optimization, and full frontend delivery (React, Ember.js)

Current Staff Engineer role at re:cinq with active AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generator expertise—direct alignment with emerging job requirement; started April 2025, showing cutting-edge technical engagement

Demonstrated technical leadership through multiple Staff roles, infrastructure setup (Docker/Kubernetes/CI systems at Altom), TDD and trunk-based development practices (RIDE Capital), and mentorship track record (workshops, courses, open-source maintenance)
concerns: Nest.js not explicitly mentioned despite being primary stack requirement; resume emphasizes raw Node.js and TypeScript foundations, which are strong but don't guarantee immediate Nest.js framework productivity

Testing framework specifics unclear—no Jest, Vitest, or Playwright cited; candidate created custom testing libraries (Trial, Fluent-Asserts) and practiced TDD, but standard framework expertise is undocumented

Authentication, authorization, and security best-practices not explicitly highlighted; while enterprise roles (RIDE Capital, Optilyz) likely involved such work, resume lacks visible security-focused project examples or depth claims

key_factors.factor: Current AI-agent and MCP infrastructure expertise
Nest.js and standard testing framework gaps

response.justification: Aisha is a strong full-stack candidate with 19 years' experience and 10+ years in TypeScript/Node.js, significantly exceeding the 6+ year requirement. Her current Staff role at re:cinq directly demonstrates AI-agent infrastructure and MCP expertise—an explicit job requirement that shows proactive engagement with technologies the role prioritizes. She brings proven architecture capability (microservices, optimization), infrastructure depth (Docker/Kubernetes/CI), and communication strength (teaching track record). Primary gaps are undocumented Nest.js and standard testing framework (Jest/Vitest/Playwright) experience—though strong Node.js/TypeScript foundations and documented TDD practices at RIDE Capital suggest these are learnable rather than fundamental misalignments. Security expertise is likely present but not visible in highlights.
